activities.name: Flight & Airport Transfer
activities.notes: Arrives 17:35. Take Narita Express (NEX) to Shinjuku Station. ~1h 30min.

activities.name: 🍴 Omoide Yokocho Memory Lane
activities.notes: Tiny yakitori stalls packed into a narrow alley near Shinjuku Station West exit.

activities.name: 🍴 Ichiran Nishi-Shinjuku
activities.notes: Solo-booth ramen — perfect for late arrival. 5-min walk from hotel.

saved.text: 🥩 Yoroniku (よろにく) — wagyu table grill, Minami-Aoyama

activities.name: JR Chuo Line: Shinjuku → Tachikawa
activities.notes: 40 min · JR Chuo Special Rapid from Shinjuku Station to Tachikawa Station, then 10 min walk north to park entrance. ¥473.

activities.name: Showa Kinen Park
activities.notes:
✅ Sleep in ok — opens 09:30, but early arrival = emptier paths.
Massive former airfield turned park in Tachikawa, ~40 min from Shinjuku. Mid-May peak: nemophila (baby blue eyes) on Herb Hill, poppies, roses. 1.6 million flowers. Herb Hill is the main photo spot — blue field stretching toward the sky. Also: rainbow pool, cycling paths, and Silver Wing fountain plaza. ¥450 entry.
activities.place: Showa Memorial Park, Tachikawa, Tokyo, Japan

activities.name: JR Chuo Line: Tachikawa → Shinjuku
activities.notes: 40 min · JR Chuo Special Rapid back to Shinjuku Station. ¥473.

activities.name: Cross Shinjuku Vision (3D Cat)
activities.notes: The iconic giant 3D cat billboard. Best viewed from across the street.

activities.name: 🍴 Tokyo Mentsu-dan
activities.notes: Ramen restaurant near Shinjuku. 10-min walk.

activities.name: Shin-Okubo (Korea Town)
activities.notes: Walk 10 min north from Shinjuku Station. Tokyo's Korea Town — street food, K-pop shops, bubble tea.

activities.name: Walk to Isetan Shinjuku
activities.notes: 13 min · 0.8 km

activities.name: Isetan Shinjuku
activities.notes: Tokyo's most famous department store. Great for Japanese cosmetics, food hall in the basement.

activities.name: Walk to Shinjuku Central Park
activities.notes: 20 min · 1.3 km · Head west past the station

activities.name: Shinjuku Central Park
activities.notes: 15-min walk or 'We Bus' from West Exit. Nice breather before the observation deck.

activities.name: Walk to Tokyo Metropolitan Gov Building
activities.notes: 6 min · 0.4 km

activities.name: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building
activities.notes: Free observation deck on the 45th floor. South Tower open this day (North closed — 2nd Monday). Incredible city views.
activities.place: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

activities.name: Walk to Kabukicho
activities.notes: 18 min · 1.2 km · Head east through Nishi-Shinjuku toward the station, then north into Kabukicho

activities.name: 🍴 Kabukicho Izakaya Dinner
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Was Omoide Yokocho again — swapped to avoid repeat. Try Torikizoku (cheap yakitori chain, fun vibe) or explore the izakaya alleys around Kabukicho for a different atmosphere than night 1.

activities.name: Godzilla Head & Arcade
activities.notes: Godzilla head on the Gracery Shinjuku hotel, then dive into Kabukicho arcades.
activities.place: Godzilla Head, Kabukicho, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

activities.name: Golden-Gai
activities.notes: Maze of tiny bars seating 5–8 people. Each has its own vibe. Perfect for a nightcap.

activities.name: Travel to PokéPark Kanto
activities.notes:
✅ Sleep in ok — park opens at 10:00, no benefit to leaving before 08:30.
⚡ ADDED: Keio Line from Shinjuku → Keio Yomiuriland Station (~25 min), then Sky Shuttle gondola up to the park. Total ~45-60 min door-to-door.

activities.name: PokéPark Kanto
activities.notes: Tickets in mail. Check confirmation for exact entry time. Park opens 10:00, plan ~5 hours: Pokémon Forest trail (~500m walk with 600+ Pokémon), Sedge Town (Pokémon Center, Gym, shops), Pika Pika Sparks show (must-see!), parades (15:55 one is less crowded than 13:20). Grab the starter character drinks early — they sell out by afternoon. No lockers inside PokéPark, travel light. Download the official PokéPark app before arriving. Your ticket includes Yomiuriland admission too.

activities.name: Travel to Shimokitazawa
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Keio Line from Keio Yomiuriland → Shimokitazawa (~20 min, transfer at Meidaimae to Keio Inokashira Line). Conveniently on the way back toward Shinjuku.

activities.name: Shimokitazawa — Vintage & Thrift Shopping
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Tokyo's bohemian neighborhood, 7 min from Shinjuku by train. Over 200 thrift/vintage shops — try New York Joe Exchange (in a former bathhouse), Chicago, Flamingo, or Stick Out (everything ¥800). Also check out Bonus Track and Mikan Shimokita complex near the station for indie cafés and restaurants.

activities.name: 🍴 Dinner in Shimokitazawa
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: The area is famous for curry (try Nijiiro Curry Shokudo or Curry Spice Gelateria KALPASI) and has great izakaya options. Also check Shirohige's Cream Puff Factory for Totoro-shaped cream puffs for dessert (official Studio Ghibli spot).

activities.name: Return to hotel
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Odakyu Line from Shimokitazawa → Shinjuku Station (~7 min). Short walk back to Kadoya Hotel.

activities.name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Moved 30 min earlier (was 09:00–10:15). Opens at 9:00, arrive early to beat crowds. 15-min walk from hotel. Beautiful mix of French, English and Japanese garden styles. Exit via Sendagaya Gate to head toward Meiji Jingu.
activities.place: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden, Tokyo, Japan

activities.name: Walk: Sendagaya Gate → Meiji Jingu
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: The walk from Gyoen's Sendagaya Gate to Meiji Jingu entrance is ~15-20 min, then another 10 min through the forested approach path to the main shrine. Enjoy the transition from garden to forest.

activities.name: ⛩ Meiji Jingu
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Extended to 1.5 hours (was 1 hour). The walk through the torii gates and forest path to the main shrine takes 15-20 min each way. Serene forest shrine in the middle of the city — take your time here.

activities.name: Walk to Takeshita Street
activities.notes: 11 min · 0.75 km · Cross Harajuku Station

activities.name: Takeshita Street
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Shifted later (was 11:30). Cross from Harajuku Station. Harajuku fashion — crepes, candy floss, quirky stores.

activities.name: 🍴 Lunch in Harajuku
activities.notes: Option A (3D Latte Art): Reissue. Option B (Dumplings): Harajuku Gyozaro.

activities.name: Walk to Cat Street
activities.notes: 11 min · 0.8 km · Head south down Meiji-dori from Harajuku Station

activities.name: Cat Street
activities.notes: Connects Harajuku to Shibuya. Boutiques, vintage shops, cafés — less crowded than Takeshita.

activities.name: Walk to Miyashita Park
activities.notes: 10 min · 0.7 km · Cat Street leads straight in

activities.name: Miyashita Park
activities.notes: End of Cat Street as you enter Shibuya. Rooftop park, skate park, shops and restaurants on multiple levels.

activities.name: Walk to SHIBUYA SKY
activities.notes: 7 min · 0.4 km

activities.name: SHIBUYA SKY
activities.notes: Book 4 weeks in advance! Open-air rooftop observation deck on top of Shibuya Scramble Square. Perfect timing for sunset (~18:30-18:45 in mid-May). NEEDS TICKET IF WANT TO GO.
activities.place: SHIBUYA SKY, Shibuya Scramble Square, Tokyo, Japan

activities.name: Shibuya Crossing
activities.notes: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing. Best viewed from Mag's Park rooftop (less crowded than the Starbucks).

activities.name: 🍴 Dinner
activities.notes: Option A: Shibuya Hikarie food floors. Option B: Shibuya Yokocho alley.
